title: 10 best herbal tinctures for inflamed gums
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for inflamed gums are concentrated plant extracts that help alleviate gum inflammation, reducing pain and discomfort. These tinctures work by soothing and protecting the gums, promoting healthy oral tissue. The best herbal tinctures for inflamed gums include Echinacea purpurea, Melaleuca alternifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Calendula officinalis, Salvia officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Commiphora molmol, Sanguinaria canadensis, Zingiber officinale, and Aloe vera. If left untreated, inflamed gums can lead to painful chewing, difficulty speaking, and even tooth loss, affecting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as calyxin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, while also inhibiting the growth of bacteria that can cause gum disease. Additionally, pot marigold's antiseptic properties help to promote a healthy oral environment by preventing infection and reducing inflammation, ultimately leading to healthier, happier gums.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Marigold's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that contribute to inflammation and tissue damage in the mouth, reducing swelling and discomfort in inflamed gums.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as lutein and zeaxanthin, reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for gingivitis and periodontal disease.

Antimicrobial properties: Marigold's antimicrobial compounds inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections and inflammation, promoting healthy oral flora and preventing further inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce swelling and prevent infection in inflamed gums.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): As powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, they help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, promoting healing and reducing discomfort.
Naringenin: This flavonoid has shown anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in inflamed gums, while also promoting wound healing.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are commonly used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe inflamed gums.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can aid in gum inflammation treatment.
Roots: They are sometimes used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers in full bloom at the peak of their potency and fragrance.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers completely to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Combine one part dried calendula flowers with one part high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain and filter the liquid tincture through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a clear liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The herb's active compounds, such as echinacin and chicoric acid, have been shown to reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, while also inhibiting the growth of bacteria that can contribute to gum inflammation. Additionally, purple coneflower's antimicrobial properties help to eliminate plaque and prevent further bacterial buildup, promoting healthy gums and a reduction in inflammation.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Echinacea's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in gum tissue, thereby alleviating swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Echinacea contains bioactive compounds like cichoric acid and echinacein, which exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce gum inflammation and prevent further irritation.

Immune system support: Purple coneflower tinctures contain immunomodulatory compounds that stimulate the immune system to fight off underlying infections or pathogens contributing to gum inflammation, promoting a healthy balance in the oral cavity.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to gum inflammation.
Triterpenoid Saponins: These saponins exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm inflamed gums by reducing redness and swelling.
Cichoric Acid: This phenolic compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the gums from further damage, reduce inflammation, and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate gum inflammation.
Leaves: They have antiseptic and antiviral properties, making them effective in treating and preventing infections that may lead to inflamed gums.
Flowers: They possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect inflamed gum tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has dried completely.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 6 hours.
Combine 1 part dried flowers and leaves with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidant-rich extract from sage leaves reduces swelling and soothes irritated gum tissue, providing relief from discomfort and pain. Additionally, sage's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria that can contribute to gum inflammation. By reducing inflammation and eliminating the source of infection, sage tinctures promote healthy gums and a strong dental foundation, ultimately leading to a reduction in symptoms associated with inflamed gums.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Sage tincture's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause gum inflammation, promoting a healthy oral environment.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums.
Antioxidant activity: Sage's antioxidant properties help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum inflammation and disease.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating inflamed gums.
Salvianolic acid: A phenolic compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory properties by suppressing the activity of inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to gum inflammation.
Carnosic acid: A phenolic diterpene that helps reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which contribute to pain and inflammation in inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Salvia officinalis leaves are used to make tinctures for inflamed gums because of their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and fight infection.
Roots: Salvia officinalis roots are used to make tinctures for inflamed gums due to their ability to reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial infections.
Buds: Salvia officinalis buds are used to make tinctures for inflamed gums as they contain antibacterial properties that help to combat gum infections and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of fresh salvia officinalis leaves and flowers at peak potency in the morning.
Chop the herb into small pieces and place it in a clean glass jar with 500ml of 70% ethanol.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and label them with the date and ingredients used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes that contribute to gum inflammation. Additionally, licorice tincture's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections that can exacerbate gum inflammation. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infection, licorice tincture can provide relief from symptoms such as redness, swelling, and bleeding, promoting overall oral health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal licorice contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which reduce inflammation in the mouth and alleviate swelling in inflamed gums.

Antimicrobial properties: Licorice extract has been shown to have antimicrobial effects against bacteria and fungi that can cause gum infections and inflammation, helping to prevent further infection and promote healing.

remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin helps with inflamed gums by reducing inflammation and soothing irritated tissues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Liquiritin: This flavonoid phenolic glycoside has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help to reduce gum inflammation and prevent further damage to gum tissue.

18α-Glycyrrhetinic acid (18α-GA): This triterpenoid saponin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce gum inflammation, prevent the progression of gum disease, and promote healing of gum tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make tinctures for inflamed gums due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their secondary compounds which are said to have anti-inflammatory properties helping with gum inflammation.
Barks: The barks are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties which help soothe and calm inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 2 parts of the roots' outer bark in early spring or fall.
Dry the harvested material in a warm and dry place for 2-4 weeks or by using a food dehydrator for 24 hours.
Use a mortar and pestle to grind 1 part of the dried material into a fine powder in 1 minute.
Combine the ground material with 70% ethanol in a 1:5 ratio in a clean glass container and seal it tightly.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the container occasionally every 2 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and antimicrobial properties. The tannins present in maidenhair tree help reduce swelling and ease pain, while the flavonoids work to combat bacteria that can contribute to gum inflammation. Additionally, the astringent properties of the tincture help constrict blood vessels, reducing bleeding and promoting healthy gum tissue regeneration. This natural remedy can provide relief from inflamed gums without harsh chemicals or artificial additives.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginkgo biloba contains a variety of flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Maidenhair Tree tinctures can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to gum inflammation and damage, promoting healthy tissue repair and reducing oxidative stress.

Vasodilatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's flavonoids can dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the gums and enhancing oxygenation of tissues, which may help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the gums, which may help alleviate inflamed gum tissue.
Terpenoids (specifically, bilobalide): Bilobalide has anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe and calm inflamed gum tissue, promoting healing and reducing pain.
Bilobetin (a type of flavonoid): Bilobetin has antioxidant properties that help protect the gums from oxidative stress, which can contribute to inflammation and damage to gum tissue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in inflamed gums.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba contain terpene lactones, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect gum tissue and reduce inflammation.
Barks: The bark of Ginkgo biloba contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in the fall season when they are fully ripe.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1 cup of the dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, then transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its soothing properties, which calm inflammation and reduce redness. The mucilage present in slippery elm works as a protective barrier, shielding the gums from irritants and bacteria while promoting healthy tissue repair. remedy_constituents: Tannins: Tannins help with inflamed gums by reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial adhesion to gum tissues, thus alleviating symptoms of gum inflammation.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Ulmus rubra exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with inflamed gums.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids found in Ulmus rubra possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect gum tissues from oxidative damage and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Barks: The inner bark of Ulmus rubra contains astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe inflamed gums.
Leaves: The leaves of Ulmus rubra have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, making them effective in treating inflamed gums and promoting oral health.
Buds: The buds of Ulmus rubra contain astringent and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and soothe inflamed tissues, including gums.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of dried ulmus rubra roots and stems and place them in a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried ulmus rubra material with 500 milliliters of 35% ethanol in the glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to separate the tincture from plant material.
Label and date the tincture and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh roots of althaea officinalis in the morning after the dew has evaporated.

Combine the chopped roots with 2 parts of high-proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest dried turmeric roots and measure out one ounce of the herb for processing.
Combine the turmeric roots with 100 proof vodka and let it macerate in a glass jar for 6 weeks.
Shake the jar daily for 30 seconds to ensure even extraction and infusion.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in an amber glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature, allowing it to mature for an additional 2 weeks before use.

This natural chemical has been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with gum inflammation. When applied topically as a tincture, eugenol can penetrate deep into the gum tissue, calming irritated areas and promoting healing. Additionally, cloves' antiseptic properties help combat bacterial buildup, further reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy gum environment. Regular use of clove tinctures can lead to a reduction in redness, swelling, and sensitivity, resulting in healthier gums.
remedy_properties: Analgesic and Anti-inflammatory properties: Cloves contain compounds like eugenol, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce pain and swelling in inflamed gums.

Antibacterial properties: The antibacterial agents in clove tinctures, such as caryophyllene, help combat the bacteria that can contribute to gum inflammation and infection, reducing the severity of symptoms.
Antioxidant properties: Clove tincture's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue, promoting healing and protecting against further inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the cloves plant contain a high concentration of essential oils, particularly eugenol, which has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to reduce swelling in the gums.
Rhyzomes: Cloves rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat various oral health issues, including gum inflammation, due to their antibacterial and analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh syzygium aromaticum bark at dawn when the sap is most potent.
Dry the harvested bark in a warm dry place for three to four weeks until brittle.
Grind one part dried syzygium aromaticum bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steep one teaspoon of the ground bark in one cup of 70% proof vodka for two weeks shaking daily.
Filter the liquid through cheesecloth and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with tight lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The oil extracted from the leaves of the Melaleuca alternifolia plant has been shown to effectively combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause gum inflammation. Additionally, tea tree tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness and swelling in the gums, promoting a healthy and pain-free oral environment. Regular use of tea tree tinctures can lead to a reduction in inflammation, fresher breath, and a lower risk of gum disease.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Tea tree tincture contains compounds like melaleuca alternifolia, which have antibacterial properties that can help combat bacterial infections in the mouth and reduce inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tannins present in tea tree tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums.
Antioxidant properties: Tea tree tincture is rich in antioxidants that can help neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress, which can contribute to gum inflammation and damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and preventing infection in inflamed gums.
Barks: Rich in terpenes and other bioactive compounds that help to soothe and calm inflamed gum tissue, promoting healing and reducing pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia in the morning and clean them thoroughly.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and combine them with 80-proof vodka at a ratio of 1:5.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for eight weeks, shaking it daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids and transfer the liquid to a clean glass bottle.
Label the tincture with the date of preparation and store it in a cool dark place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The extract from the flowers of the plant contains compounds such as linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gums. Additionally, lavender's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria that can cause gum inflammation, promoting a healthy oral environment. By reducing inflammation and combating infection, English lavender tinctures provide a natural solution for soothing inflamed gums and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial activity: Lavender's antibacterial properties help combat bacterial infections in the mouth, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender, such as linalool, reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums by inhibiting prostaglandins and other inflammatory mediators.
Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Lavender's calming effects can help alleviate anxiety and stress-related gum inflammation, promoting a healthier oral environment and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender are used to make tinctures that help reduce inflammation and soothe gum pain due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.

Seeds: Lavender seeds contain compounds like camphor and linalool, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in reducing gum inflammation and promoting healthy oral tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 100g of the ground lavender flowers with 600ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to ensure all parts are fully saturated with the vodka.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and reserving the tincture.
Store the finished tincture in a cool dark place for at least 6 weeks before using it to help alleviate inflamed gums, taking 10-15 drops three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The natural salicin in white willow bark has been shown to reduce pain and swelling, making it an effective remedy for gum inflammation. When applied topically, the tincture can penetrate deep into the gum tissue, soothing irritated areas and promoting healing. Regular use of herbal white willow tinctures can help alleviate symptoms of gingivitis, periodontitis, and other gum-related conditions, leading to a healthier and more comfortable smile.
remedy_properties: Salicin: White willow tinctures contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce pain and inflammation by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, resulting in relief from gum pain and swelling.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in white willow tinctures help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to gum inflammation and damage, promoting healthy gums and a reduced risk of periodontal disease.

Anti-inflammatory flavonoids: White willow contains flavonoids such as salicin and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with inflamed gums, while also supporting the body's natural healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the white willow plant contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain in inflamed gums.
Barks: The bark of the white willow contains salicin as well, making it a popular choice for creating tinctures that can help alleviate gum inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salix alba leaves and stems by cutting them from mature trees with clean scissors or pruning shears.
Weigh out one pound of harvested material and place it into a clean glass jar.
Fill the jar with 100 proof vodka to completely cover the plant material, ensuring a 1:4 ratio.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing for thorough maceration.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining only the tincture.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to inflamed gums that are treated with herbal tinctures are conditions characterized by inflammation, pain, and discomfort in the mouth or throat. Examples include aphthous ulcers, oral thrush, and tonsillitis. Herbal tinctures such as Calendula, Echinacea, and Slippery Elm have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help to reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote healing in these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with inflamed gums is a blend of Calendula, Echinacea, and Myrrh. Calendula's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling, while Echinacea's immune-boosting properties combat underlying infections. Myrrh's antimicrobial properties prevent bacterial growth, promoting healing. These tinctures work synergistically to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote gum health. A typical ratio is 1:1:1 Calendula to Echinacea to Myrrh, taken 2-3 times a day, diluted in water or added to mouthwash for optimal results. Consult a healthcare professional before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The tincture's immune-boosting qualities also help combat the underlying infection causing inflammation. Additionally, Echinacea's antimicrobial properties prevent the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that contribute to gum disease. By addressing the root causes of inflamed gums, Echinacea purpurea tinctures promote healthy gum tissue and overall oral well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for gum inflammation and gum disease.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: They help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Caffeic acid: It acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce gum inflammation and promote healing.
Polysaccharides: They stimulate the immune system to produce more white blood cells, which helps to fight off infections and reduce inflammation in the gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in antioxidants and contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating inflamed gums.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation and fight off infections, making them beneficial for gum health.
Flowers: They contain alkylamides and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and protect inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried echinacea purpurea root and flowers in a ratio of 2:1 for tincture preparation purposes.
Chop the echinacea root and flowers into smaller pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped echinacea root and flowers with 80 proof vodka at a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Steep the echinacea mixture in the vodka for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the echinacea tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in this herbal remedy, such as cineole and limonene, have been shown to effectively combat bacteria and reduce swelling in the gums. This natural treatment promotes a healthy balance of oral flora, reducing redness and pain associated with inflamed gums. Regular use of Melaleuca alternifolia tinctures can help alleviate gum inflammation and promote overall oral well-being.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene helps to reduce inflammation and ease pain in inflamed gums due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Limonene: This terpene has antimicrobial properties that help to combat the underlying causes of gum inflammation, such as bacterial infections.
Cafartic acid (Caffeic acid): This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high oil content, particularly eucalyptol, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Stems of Melaleuca alternifolia contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and fight bacterial infections.
Barks: The bark is used due to its antimicrobial properties, helping to combat bacteria that cause gum inflammation and infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh leaves from melaleuca alternifolia plants in early morning for optimal oil content.
Chop 2 parts of fresh leaves and add them to a clean glass jar for steeping.
Add 1 part of 80-proof vodka to the jar to create a 1:2 leaf-to-alcohol ratio and seal it.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow for extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store it.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tincture's active compound, eucalyptol, has been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with gum inflammation. Additionally, eucalyptol has a natural antiseptic property that helps combat bacterial infections, promoting a healthy environment for gum recovery. By applying the tincture directly to the affected area, individuals can experience relief from inflamed gums and promote overall oral health. Regular use may also prevent future gum inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These antioxidants help reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria that cause gum inflammation.
Cineole: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums.
Flavonoids: These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help prevent infection and promote healing in inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of eucalyptol, a key compound responsible for the anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe inflamed gums.
Barks: Eucalyptus bark is rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds that exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating gum inflammation.
Roots: Eucalyptus roots contain various bioactive compounds, including sesquiterpenes, that possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves from a pesticide-free source in late summer.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 95-105 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 hours.
Combine the dried leaves with 80-proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio by weight for a 4-6 week maceration period.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after allowing it to steep for 4-6 weeks.
Store the tincture in dark glass bottles away from direct sunlight and heat for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. When applied topically, Calendula tinctures can soothe and calm inflamed gums, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help prevent infection, promoting a healthy oral environment and facilitating the healing process. This makes Calendula a popular natural remedy for gum inflammation and related oral health issues.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These constituents exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with inflamed gums.

Flavonoids (including quercetin and kaempferol): As antioxidants, flavonoids help combat oxidative stress, which can contribute to inflammation and gum damage, while also promoting healing and tissue repair.

Phenolic acids (including caffeic and ferulic acids): These compounds possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing bacterial growth and inflammation in the gums, promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help in soothing and healing inflamed gums.
Leaves: They are utilized for their ability to reduce inflammation and fight bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation.
Roots: They are employed for their potential to reduce swelling and promote healing of inflamed gums due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of fresh calendula officinalis flowers and 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka.
Combine the flowers and vodka in a clean glass jar and stir to ensure the flowers are covered.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to a dark glass dropper bottle and store it in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tannins present in Salvia officinalis have been shown to reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with gum inflammation. Additionally, the antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation. The antimicrobial properties of Salvia officinalis also inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections, promoting a healthy balance of oral flora and contributing to the reduction of inflamed gums. This natural remedy provides relief from gum inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease pain in inflamed gums.
Carnosic acid: This phenolic diterpene has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to protect gum tissue from damage and reduce inflammation.
Ursolic acid: This triterpenoid has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to combat bacterial infections that may cause inflamed gums and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents.
Stems: Stems are also used, as they contain a significant amount of essential oils and flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
Roots: Roots are sometimes used in tinctures, as they are rich in triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves and flowers on a sunny day.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar to prepare for maceration.
Add 100ml of high-proof vodka or glycerin to the jar at a 1:5 plant-to-solvent ratio for maceration.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking to aid extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to filter the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing. By applying the tincture to affected areas, it can help to calm irritated tissues and alleviate pain, promoting a healthy environment for the gums to recover. This natural remedy can provide relief from swollen and tender gums.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone derivative has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in inflamed gums.
Catechins: These flavonoid compounds have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help soothe and calm inflamed gums, reducing redness and swelling.

Vitamins and minerals (especially Vitamin C and Calcium): These nutrients help promote collagen synthesis, wound healing, and tissue repair in inflamed gums, reducing the risk of further complications.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and calm inflamed gums.
Leaves: The gel from the leaves contains aloin, which has astringent properties that can help reduce swelling and ease pain in inflamed gums.

Leaves: The extracts from Aloe barbadensis leaves have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making them effective in treating gum inflammation and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100% pure aloe barbadensis leaves from a trusted source with thick green skin.
Chop 2 pounds of the harvested leaves into small pieces to facilitate efficient extraction.
Combine the chopped aloe barbadensis with 80% vodka in a 1:2 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a glass container with a lid for 2-3 weeks shaking daily.
Strain and discard the solids then transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Commiphora molmol tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tincture is derived from the resin of the Commiphora molmol tree, which has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various oral health issues. The resin contains compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth, thereby promoting a healthy gum environment. Regular use of Commiphora molmol tinctures may help alleviate symptoms of inflamed gums, such as redness and swelling, and promote overall oral well-being.
remedy_constituents: Boswellic acids: They help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.
Terpenoids: They possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in inflamed gums.
Alkane triterpenoids: They exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help to soothe and calm inflamed gums, reducing pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Commiphora molmol contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation and soothe painful gums.
Rhyzomes: Commiphora molmol rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat gum inflammation due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried commiphora molmol bark and roots in the early morning when the plant is at its peak potency.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area for efficient solvent extraction.
Combine 250ml of 80% ethanol with the chopped plant material in a clean glass jar for a 1:5 ratio.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2 weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to separate the liquid tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sanguinaria canadensis tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as sanguinarine and dihydrosanguinarine, have been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause gum infections. By decreasing inflammation and eliminating the source of infection, Sanguinaria canadensis tinctures promote healthy gum tissue and alleviate symptoms associated with inflamed gums, including pain, swelling, and redness. This natural remedy can be a valuable addition to oral care routines.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: This alkaloid has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums.
Sanguinarine: This alkaloid has antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacteria that can cause gum infections and inflammation.
Palmatine: This alkaloid has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation in inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high concentration of isoquinoline alkaloids, particularly sanguinarine, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also used, as they contain sanguinarine and other alkaloids with analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe inflamed gums.
Barks: The barks may be used to some extent, as they contain some of the same alkaloids found in the roots and leaves, offering similar anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial benefits.
remedy_recipe: Gather sanguinaria canadensis roots and leaves in their entirety for tincture making purposes.
Combine 1 part of the roots and leaves with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The active compound gingerol in Zingiber officinale has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to gum inflammation. As a result, Zingiber officinale tincture may be a natural and effective solution for alleviating inflamed gums and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and ease pain in inflamed gums.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols possess anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with inflamed gums.
Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial infections that can cause inflammation in the gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a high concentration of gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties to combat gum inflammation.
Buds: The buds of the Zingiber officinale plant contain a concentrated form of ginger's active compounds, which can be used to create a potent tincture to soothe inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh rhizomes in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Clean the rhizomes by gently scrubbing them with a vegetable brush for 5 minutes.
Chop the rhizomes into small pieces and macerate them in 500ml of 30-50% ethanol for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow the flavors to mature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tinctures helps with inflamed gums because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated tissues. The gel of the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation. Additionally, Aloe vera tinctures have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial overgrowth in the mouth, further aiding in the reduction of inflammation and promoting a healthy oral environment. This makes Aloe vera tinctures a natural and effective remedy for inflamed gums.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help with inflamed gums by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing through their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Polysaccharides: Help with inflamed gums by stimulating the immune system and promoting the production of collagen, which helps in tissue repair and regeneration.
Anthraquinones: Help with inflamed gums by acting as anti-inflammatory agents and reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines that contribute to gum inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of Aloe vera's medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe vera gel from mature leaves using sharp knives or pruning shears to minimize damage.
Dry the aloe vera gel in a dehydrator at 135 degrees fahrenheit for 6 hours.
Combine 1 part aloe vera gel with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter into another glass container discarding the solids.
